name: Cruzado Walks and Locks (in Shadow Position)
preceding_figure_names: Foot Change 1, Foot Change 8, Maypole, Natural Basic Movement, Reverse Basic Movement, Shadow Circular Volta with LF, Shadow Circular Volta with RF, Shadow Traveling Volta commenced with LF, Shadow Traveling Volta commenced with RF, Side Basic Movement
following_figure_names: Shadow Circular Volta with LF, Shadow Circular Volta with RF, Foot Change 2, Foot Change 4
notes:
Hold may be released, adjusting to Right Side Position.
Cruzados Walks and Locks are normally danced facing LOD from a Foot Change to Right Shadow Position (Foot Change # 1 or 8) or any figure that may be danced in Right Shadow Position.
Cruzados Walks are the only SQQ timing that has bounce action.
The bounce action in Cruzado walk (one high hip and one low hip) is different from normal Samba bounce (hip/pelvis fwd on whole count).
When collecting legs, bring thighs together, no light through thighs (e.g. when doing the locks, and when legs are passing).
When stepping forward, don't go up and then down (too much rise). Focus on body moving forward.
When stepping forward, do not cross foot when doing Walks, keep 2 lines. Cross foot only for Locks.
Don't go too low with the front knee (which hurts knee in long run).
Open the back hip to maximum, finishing feet and rotation before collecting.
Chest fwd, don't go down. Chest and hip are stretch opposite.
Two or four Cruzados Walks may precede or follow Cruzados Locks.

steps.action: Using pressure through ball of LF, release L heel and start to straighten knees, moving RF forward on toe until it is level with LF, while moving pelvis slightly forward.
Continue straightening knees, moving RF forward on ball of foot, arriving at the top of the bounce with R heel lightly in contact with the floor, weight central, both legs straight but not braced.
comments: Finish the hip behind (rotate more) and bend front knee more for a fuller movement.
Absorb the weight into RF/R knee, ribs more forward (not pelvis more forward).

steps.action: Transfer weight forward to RF, flexing the knee, reaching the lowest point of the bounce, allowing the back foot and leg to turn out and complete a rotational hip movement.
comments: Retain tone in back leg with pressure on inside edge of toe.
